question: Design a `Department` class that holds a collection of `Professor` objects (aggregation). Implement methods to `hireProfessor()`, `fireProfessor()`, and `displayProfessors()`.

test_cases.input:
hire Alice Smith 101
hire Bob Johnson 102
hire Charlie Brown 103
hire Alice Smith 101
display
fire 102
fire 105
display
hire Frank White 104
display
fire 101
fire 103
fire 104
display
test_cases.expected:
Hired Professor Alice Smith (ID: 101) to Computer Science
Hired Professor Bob Johnson (ID: 102) to Computer Science
Hired Professor Charlie Brown (ID: 103) to Computer Science
Error: Professor with ID 101 is already in the department.

--- Computer Science Department Professors (3) ---
  ID: 101, Name: Alice Smith
  ID: 102, Name: Bob Johnson
  ID: 103, Name: Charlie Brown
--------------------------------------

--- Firing professors from Computer Science ---
Fired Professor with ID 102 from Computer Science
Error: Professor with ID 105 not found in Computer Science.

--- Computer Science Department Professors (2) ---
  ID: 101, Name: Alice Smith
  ID: 103, Name: Charlie Brown
--------------------------------------
Hired Professor Frank White (ID: 104) to Computer Science

--- Computer Science Department Professors (3) ---
  ID: 101, Name: Alice Smith
  ID: 103, Name: Charlie Brown
  ID: 104, Name: Frank White
--------------------------------------

--- Firing remaining professors ---
Fired Professor with ID 101 from Computer Science
Fired Professor with ID 103 from Computer Science
Fired Professor with ID 104 from Computer Science

--- Computer Science Department Professors (0) ---
  No professors currently in this department.
--------------------------------------

driver: The `main` function serves as the driver. It creates a `Department` object and demonstrates all specified functionalities: hiring professors (including attempts to hire duplicates), displaying the current list of professors, and firing professors (both existing and non-existent ones) with clear feedback for each operation.
